steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Both examples follow the same path: an Indigenous language (Nahuatl or Quechua) to Spanish, then Spanish to English. The question asks which claim these examples best support.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It has served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English.: Both coyote and condor entered English via Spanish, making Spanish the intermediary. ✓
  • Choice B — Its contribution to English vocabulary roughly equals the collective contribution by Indigenous languages.: The examples show Spanish as an intermediate step, not direct borrowing. ✗
  • Choice C — It has borrowed words from Indigenous languages and contributed words to them.: The borrowing runs the opposite direction in the passage. ✗
  • Choice D — It adopted Nahuatl and Quechua words in approximately equal numbers.: No claim about the relationship between these languages is made. ✗
That matches Choice A — It has served as a medium through which Indigenous languages have influenced English..
